name: Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones – circular by the rivers Huerna and Pajares
name_original: Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones – circular por los ríos Huerna y Pajares
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lena is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation changes and routes that traverse river valleys and high passes. The region offers a diverse landscape, from dense woodlands to open mountain slopes. Riders can expect challenging climbs and descents, with trails often following natural contours and offering expansive views. This area is known for its rugged beauty and provides a robust environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Lena The most popular mountain bike trail is Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones – circular by the rivers Huerna and Pajares, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ride through river valleys and mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Monument to the Maquis loop from Pola de Lena, a difficult 39.2 miles (63.0 km) path. This route features significant climbs and descents, providing panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the La Cubilla Pass – Campomanes loop from Campomanes, a 36.3 miles (58.5 km) trail leading through mountainous passes and valleys, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Lena is defined by high mountain passes, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options primarily for experienced riders, with 18 difficult routes and 4 moderate routes available. The routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 230 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lena?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Lena, offering a diverse range of experiences. The majority, 18 routes, are classified as difficult, with 4 moderate options also available.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Lena?
A: Mountain biking around Lena is primarily suited for experienced riders due to its mountainous terrain, significant elevation changes, and challenging climbs and descents. Most routes are classified as difficult, with a few moderate options available.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Lena?
A: Yes, many routes in Lena are circular. For example, you can try the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485828/pola-de-lena-campumanes-camino-buzan-los-pontones-circular-by-the-rivers-huerna-and-pajares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones – circular by the rivers Huerna and Pajares&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass – Campomanes loop from Campomanes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Lena?
A: Riders can expect to traverse diverse landscapes, from dense woodlands to open mountain slopes. Trails often follow natural contours, offering expansive views, river valleys, and high mountain passes. The region is known for its rugged beauty.

Q: What are some notable mountain passes or summits I might encounter?
A: The region features several prominent mountain passes and summits. You might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or even reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/825310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Gamoniteiro&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueto Negro Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Lena typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485828/pola-de-lena-campumanes-camino-buzan-los-pontones-circular-by-the-rivers-huerna-and-pajares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones circular&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 4 hours 16 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20498708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Monument to the Maquis loop&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Lena?
A: The routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 12 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the challenging terrain, panoramic views, and the rugged beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any specific trails with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, many trails feature significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de Lena – Alto de Gamoniteiro loop from Pola de Lena&lt;/a&gt; involves over 2000 meters of ascent, offering a very demanding ride.

Q: Can I find trails that pass through historical or cultural landmarks?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20498708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Monument to the Maquis loop&lt;/a&gt; includes a monument, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical context.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Lena?
A: One of the longest routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20498708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Monument to the Maquis loop from Pola de Lena&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 39.2 miles (63.0 km) and features significant climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of river valleys?
A: Yes, several trails traverse river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485828/pola-de-lena-campumanes-camino-buzan-los-pontones-circular-by-the-rivers-huerna-and-pajares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Lena, Campumanes, Camino Buzan, Los Pontones – circular by the rivers Huerna and Pajares&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a challenging ride through the Huerna and Pajares river valleys.
tips: Section of the Camino de Santiago through the forest near Erós. The path passes under the foliage of chestnut and beech trees between the stone walls of the meadows.

An Asturian pre-Romanesque church, built in the middle of the 9th century. Unesco declared the Church of Santa Cristina de Lena a World Heritage Site in December 1985. The church can be visited from Tuesday to Sunday. It is closed on Mondays. The month of November is closed for holidays. The most comfortable pedestrian access is a road whose transit is restricted to wheeled vehicles, except for residents, property owners, and services.
